title: hieracium murorum subsp. submacropterum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. submacropterum, also known as a subspecies of the Common Hawk's-beard plant, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. submacropterum include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting digestive health and immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
